entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The primary cereal crop studied for shoot fly damage.

entities.name: ATHERIGONA SOCCATA
entities.description: The shoot fly pest that causes damage to sorghum.

entities.description: The geographic region where the research was conducted.

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE MANIPULATION
entities.description: The process of adjusting soil water levels to control pest incidence.

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: The state of reduced water availability induced in seedlings.

entities.name: RAINOUT SHELTER
entities.description: A controlled environment used to exclude rainfall.

entities.name: FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: The natural outdoor environment used for crop growth.

entities.name: LEAF SURFACE WETNESS
entities.description: The presence of water droplets on the central whorl leaf of seedlings.

entities.name: IS 1054
entities.description: A moderately resistant genotype of sorghum.

entities.name: OVIPOSITION
entities.description: The process of egg-laying by the shoot fly.

entities.name: DEADHEARTS
entities.description: The symptom of shoot fly damage where the central leaf whorl dies.

entities.name: PLANT BIOMASS
entities.description: The total biological mass of the sorghum plant.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of grain produced per unit area.

entities.name: INSECTICIDES
entities.description: Chemical agents used to control the shoot fly infestation.

entities.name: FULL IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of maximum required water to the crop.
